_doc: Paradata registry (London Charter / Seville Principles): the reasoning behind modeled or inferred choices. Facts reference these by id in `paradataRef` instead of repeating prose.

pd-gallery-gradient.rationale: The gallery is modeled near-level at ~0.1% (10 cm per 100 m), the documented constant fall from P9 to P-5, with the two documented stepped falls (1 m under P4, 1.2 m under P6). Step magnitudes are ~1 m and therefore sub-visible at scene scale, so they are shown as annotated markers rather than literal geometry.
pd-gallery-gradient.author: project team

pd-schematic-positions.rationale: Per-shaft plan positions (modelPos) are read as relative along-route spacing from fig.3-1 (the 3D block model, which carries a 100 m scale bar) and cross-checked against fig.5-3 (schematic longitudinal section) and the documented inter-shaft distances (see gallery.documentedDistances). The along-route scale is calibrated at ~7 m per model x-unit via the documented P2–P3 = 28 m (modelPos x 4→8). Under that scale the auxiliary shafts P-5A/P-7A were re-spaced (2026-07-02) so P-7A sits ~25 m NW of P-5 as documented (previously ~45 m — the over-spacing flagged in review). fig.3-1 is an oblique 3D perspective, so spacing elsewhere is approximate, NOT georeferenced. Positions are declared in a `model-schematic` CRS; real WGS84 coordinates (geo) are left null (see pd-source-precedence). Correction: these positions were previously mis-cited to fig.5-4, which is actually a photograph of shaft P2.
pd-schematic-positions.basis: Brochure 2018, fig.3-1 (p.7, 100 m scale bar); fig.5-3 (p.17); documented distances p.17, p.22
pd-schematic-positions.author: project team

pd-source-precedence.rationale: Source-of-truth hierarchy for this model: (1) the official SIT brochure (Faber/Waringo/Werner 2018) is authoritative for ALL qanat facts — shaft depths, surveyed floor elevations, gallery gradient and steps, geology, hydrology, and the route/spacing arrangement; (2) EU-DEM 25 m (OpenTopoData) is external CONTEXT terrain only — it supplies the surrounding landscape surface, not the qanat, and where it disagrees with the brochure the brochure wins; (3) OpenStreetMap shaft nodes are the lowest-authority, provisional cross-check. Critically, the brochure contains NO coordinate grid on any figure (fig.3-1 has only a 100 m scale bar on an oblique 3D view; fig.5-3 is explicitly exaggerated), so true WGS84 georeferencing CANNOT be derived from it. Therefore per-shaft `geo` stays null and OSM remains the only (provisional) georeference candidate — see pd-osm-georef. This is why the EU-DEM surface at OSM points does not reconcile with the brochure's documented depths: they are different, unmerged sources at different authority levels, not an error to 'fix' in the qanat data.
pd-source-precedence.author: project team

pd-postulated-shafts.rationale: Shafts P-2, P-3 and P-6A are inferred from the regular inter-shaft spacing of the located shafts (in qanats the spacing often matches the depth). They are not excavated or surveyed; existence is `inferred`, position `hypothetical` and schematic.
pd-postulated-shafts.author: project team

pd-nominal-datum.rationale: Vertical authority is the brochure: the surveyed shaft-floor elevations (P-7A 354.3, P-5A 357.5, P-4 355.4 m a.s.l.) and the documented depths are the true datum for the qanat. For non-GeoData terrain modes there is no real surface datum, so a nominal site datum is used (valley ~340 m, plateau ~385 m a.s.l.) purely to place the gallery/shafts at plausible depths. GeoData mode draws the SURFACE from EU-DEM metres (context), but the qanat depths/floors remain those documented by the brochure (see pd-source-precedence).
pd-nominal-datum.basis: Brochure 2018 (floors/depths); nominal surface consistent with Brochure 2018 elevations 350-405 m a.s.l.
pd-nominal-datum.author: project team

pd-osm-georef.rationale: Real shaft coordinates were imported from OpenStreetMap (data/osm-shafts.json, source osm-qanat-shafts) as candidate georeferencing. The OSM nodes are named on a '-6..9' scheme that does NOT map 1:1 to the brochure P-labels (P-7A/P-5A/P-5/P-4/P-1/P0/P1..P9), and the OSM 'P2'->'P3' spacing reads ~20 m against a documented 28 m — so the label assignment needed reconciliation. RECONCILIATION 2026-07-01 (method): (1) fig.3-1 (p.7, oblique 3D block model, the only positional figure, carrying a 100 m scale bar) was digitised — the 15 red shaft poles' base x/y pixel positions were detected and the scale bar measured at 311 px = 100 m (0.322 m/px in the foreground plane), giving along-route inter-shaft distances; (2) these were compared with the OSM chain (haversine between consecutive nodes) and the documented distances (P2-P3 = 28 m p.17; P-5->P-7A ~25 m NW p.22), and a similarity fit (scale+offset) was run mapping the OSM cumulative chain onto the digitised cumulative positions to expose residuals / off-by-one shifts; (3) the 0.5 m ACT LiDAR 2019 surface at each OSM point was checked against fig.4-2's W->E surface shape. FINDINGS: The off-by-one test is decisive — aligning the 12 OSM-mapped nodes to the CURRENT labels (P-4..P9) gives RMS 5.8 m, vs 10.8-13.9 m for any ±1/±2/±3 shift, so the OVERALL mapping is correct and there is no systematic label offset. Per-leg (digitised fig.3-1 | OSM | documented): P2->P3 24.4 | 19.8 | 28 m; P3->P4 32.2 | 35.7; P4->P5 32.0 | 31.7; P5->P6 23.5 | 23.7; P6->P7 16.4 | 16.4; P7->P8 20.1 | 20.5; P8->P9 21.3 | 22.3; P-5->P-4 12.3 | 17.9; P-4->P-1 41 | 57.4; P-1->P0 14.0 | 19.1; P0->P1 11.3 | 15.6; P1->P2 14.3 | 23.5. LiDAR profile W->E (366.8, 365.5, 365.2, 366.9, 374.2, 379.4, 386.1, 391.8, 392.5, 391.7, 392.1, 382.1, 378.2, 377.1, 377.2 m) reproduces fig.4-2 exactly: Alzette hillside ~365, monotonic rise to the flat Pëtschend plateau top (P2-P5 ~392), sharp -10 m drop at P6 into the Haedchen depression (P7-P9 ~377) — no elevation outlier signals a mis-label. CONFIDENCE TIERS: HIGH = P3, P4, P5 (plateau top: digitised, OSM and documented spacings all consistent, small fit residuals, flat-plateau LiDAR) and P6, P7, P8, P9 (Haedchen: digitised and OSM inter-shaft distances agree to ~1 m and LiDAR captures the drop — though this is the FAR/EAST end of the oblique figure where perspective foreshortening is worst, so their agreement, not the absolute digitised metres, is what carries the confidence). MEDIUM = P-4, P-1, P0, P1 (route-bend / plateau-edge zone: labels corroborated by LiDAR and sequence but the oblique foreshortening and the long P-4->P-1 leg leave spacing uncertain; P-4 is the single largest fit residual, +12.7 m). LOW = P2 (CONFLICT: OSM makes P1->P2 = 23.5 m the LONGER leg while the brochure says P2 is 'unusually close to P1' p.16-17, and OSM P2->P3 = 19.8 m is well short of the documented 28 m; P2 is the largest negative fit residual, -11 m — the OSM 'P2' node reads ~1/3 too far east) and P-7A, P-5A, P-5 (west-end auxiliary branch: P-7A/P-5A are DERIVED offsets, not OSM nodes, and P-5 rests on the OSM '-5' node while the two westernmost OSM nodes '-6'/'-5' carry no independent label, mapsTo=null; the main line and the separate ~25-30 m northern auxiliary channel per fig.5-14 p.22 cannot be disentangled from OSM alone). CAVEAT: fig.3-1 is an oblique 3D perspective, so all digitised metres are APPROXIMATE and increasingly foreshortened toward the east; they support/refute labels relatively but are not a georeference. LiDAR-at-OSM still reproduces the brochure floor+depth surfaces to 0.5-2.5 m and resolves the Haedchen drop, so the coordinates remain the best provisional georeference; the earlier EU-DEM mismatch (+10-21 m) was a 25 m plateau-edge averaging artifact. NO coordinates were re-mapped in this pass (no unambiguous alternative for the low-confidence nodes) — they are flagged only. RECOMMENDATION: a definitive fix (especially for P2 and the west-end auxiliary shafts) requires the certified SIT/CNRA survey plan the community OSM trace was drawn from (agreed plan step 4, out of scope here).
pd-osm-georef.basis: OpenStreetMap contributors (ODbL); Brochure 2018 fig.3-1 (p.7, 100 m scale bar) digitised; documented distances p.17, p.22; fig.4-2 (p.15); cross-checked against ACT LiDAR 2019 (act-lidar-2019)
pd-osm-georef.author: project team

pd-geology-digitised.rationale: Strata thicknesses were digitised from fig.4-3 (the schematic XY cross-section of the Pëtschend horst) by colour-classifying a vertical scan of the published raster against its 20 m vertical scale bar (~6.8 px/m): Liassic package (li2 sandstone + li1 marl) ≈ 50 m at the crest, Keuper mudstone (ko) ≈ 5 m (consistent across the section), Keuper dolomitic marl (km3) ≥ 44 m (base below the section). Accuracy is ~±3 m — the figure is 'schematic' per its own caption, so these are reconstructed, not surveyed. The li2/li1 internal split is not cleanly separable on the raster; the model derives the sandstone thickness from (LiDAR surface − sandstone/marl interface) instead.
pd-geology-digitised.author: project team

pd-strata-render.rationale: The geological cross-section is a mix of sourced facts and display approximation. Sourced: the stratigraphic sequence and names (Luxembourg sandstone / marl+limestone / Keuper mudstone / dolomitic marl, fig.4-3), the ~2% SE dip (p.14), and that groundwater accumulates at the sandstone/marl interface and the gallery floor sits at that boundary (p.6, p.14, fig.5-24). Rendered faithfully: in GeoData mode the sandstone/marl interface (= groundwater table) is drawn ~2 m below the near-level gallery floor at REAL elevation, and the weathered cover uses the cited ~2 m. Now digitised (pd-geology-digitised, fig.4-3): the marl (li1 ≈ 15 m below the interface) and Keuper mudstone (ko ≈ 5 m) sub-thicknesses; km3 fills to the block base (≥44 m, base below section). Still approximate: the exact interface dip and the li2/li1 internal split (derived instead from LiDAR surface − interface). Simplification: the single groundwater line drawn is the DRAINED (post-construction) interface, rendered ~flat along the W→E gradient; the pre-construction PERCHED water table (mounded between shafts) is a separate optional overlay, and the true ~2% SE bedding dip is suppressed on the section for clarity (fig.5-03).
pd-strata-render.author: project team

pd-overflow-channel.rationale: The P-4 overflow weir and its lateral (deviation) channel are documented in plan + cross-section (fig.5-11, fig.5-14) with a Ø 80 cm channel that carries ~80 m³/d when the main channel dams above ~12 cm. The brochure gives no georeferenced route for the lateral channel, so it is modeled SCHEMATICALLY as a short branch diverging from the main gallery at P-4 (shown only when the overflow-channel option is enabled), distinct from the main channel and from the higher, unconnected auxiliary dry channel (P-7A/P-5A).
pd-overflow-channel.author: project team

pd-aux-system.rationale: P-5A and P-7A belong to a separate, higher, dry auxiliary channel ~30 m north of the main line; their channel floor sits above the aquiferous qanat channel, so they were never connected to it and are drawn as a distinct dashed channel.
pd-aux-system.author: project team

pd-werner-sit-survey.rationale: Henri Werner (brochure2018 co-author, site surveyor) provided a survey table ('coordonnées Raschpetzer') giving LUREF (EPSG:2169) X/Y — and, for most points, a ground-surface Z (m a.s.l.) — for the shaft CENTREPOINT of the main line P-1 through P9 (11 points). Converted to WGS84 (pyproj, EPSG:2169→EPSG:4326) and applied to supersede the OSM/similarity-fit reconciliation (pd-osm-georef) for these 11 shafts; the western/auxiliary shafts P-4/P-5/P-5A/P-7A are NOT in this table and remain OSM-derived. COMPARISON vs. the prior OSM-derived positions: P1, P4-P9 shift <1 m (validates OSM was already accurate there); P-1 and P0 (marked '~', approximate markings, not centrepoint surveys) shift ~2.2-3.3 m; P3 (surface-depression centre, still backfilled) shifts 4.9 m; P2 (surface-depression centre) shifts 10.9 m — closely matching pd-osm-georef's own finding that 'the OSM P2 node reads ~one-third too far east'. VALIDATION: the new P2-P3 planar distance (LUREF) is 28.75 m, matching the documented 'P3 exactly on the P1-P5 line, 28 m from P2' (p.17) far more closely than the prior OSM-derived spacing (~20 m) — resolving the SSOT's long-standing spacing-invariant warning. CORRECTION NOTE: the source table's P5 X-value was transcribed without a decimal point ('78758549'); read here as 78758.549 m, the only placement consistent with the ~24-32 m shaft-to-shaft spacing established by every neighbouring leg (P3-P4=31.4 m, P4-P5=31.6 m, P5-P6=23.8 m) — flagged as an editorial correction, not a literal transcription.
pd-werner-sit-survey.basis: Henri Werner, unpublished SIT survey table + CAD site plans, provided directly to this project (2026-07-20); cross-checked against Brochure 2018 p.17 (documented P2-P3 = 28 m) and pd-osm-georef
pd-werner-sit-survey.author: project team
